hypothesis: Continual pre-training of LLMs on chemical corpora augmented with computed coarse-grained 3D structural descriptors of MOFs (e.g., accessible surface area, pore volume, metal-node coordination numbers, and topology indices) will significantly reduce mean absolute error (MAE) on QMOF band-gap prediction compared to pre-training on textual-only corpora when fine-tuned with fewer than 5,000 supervised samples.
